normal_cases.name: `import sisyphus` exposes the library API from local `sisyphus` implementations.
Legacy imports for moved core modules such as `taskflow.api`, `taskflow.agents`, `taskflow.workflow`, and `taskflow.templates` resolve to `sisyphus` implementations.

edge_cases.name: Template resource lookup succeeds from canonical `sisyphus` package data after the module move.
Compatibility-sensitive names remain unchanged where persistence depends on them, including `.taskflow.toml` fallback handling and `taskflow.event.v1`.

exception_cases.name: Focused regressions across taskflow, event bus, MCP, and golden fixtures catch import or packaging breakage before the next migration slice lands.

verification_methods.method: env PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache ./.venv/bin/python -c "import sisyphus, taskflow, sisyphus.templates, taskflow.agents, taskflow.api; print(sisyphus.__version__); print(taskflow.request_task is sisyphus.request_task); print(taskflow.agents.__name__); print(taskflow.api.__name__); print(sisyphus.templates.template_root().name)"

verification_methods.target: Legacy taskflow core imports resolve to sisyphus implementations
Event bus, MCP surface, and golden fixtures still pass after the package inversion
verification_methods.method: env PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache ./.venv/bin/python -m unittest tests.test_event_bus tests.test_mcp_server tests.test_golden -v
